# UC-12.7.2 · Argo CD Notification Service Delivery Failure Rate

## Description

Argo CD’s notification controller turns sync and health transitions into operator signals; when deliveries fail, on-call teams learn about broken deployments late or not at all. Measuring failure rate per destination isolates broken webhooks, expired tokens, and rate-limited chat integrations.

## Value

Restores trustworthy GitOps alerting so incidents are acknowledged while rollbacks are still cheap.

## Implementation

Ship notifications controller logs with component tags to Splunk. Extract destination_service (slack, teams, webhook URL host) and trigger (on-sync-failed, on-health-degraded). Tune thresholds per integration; exclude planned maintenance windows using a lookup.

## SPL

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="argocd:server" (component=notifications OR logger="*notifications*")
| eval failed=if(match(_raw,"(?i)failed to send|delivery failed|non-ok status|error sending|retry exhausted"),1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as attempts, sum(failed) as failures by _time, destination_service, trigger
| eval fail_rate=round(100*failures/nullif(attempts,0),2)
| where fail_rate > 5 AND attempts >= 10
| sort -fail_rate
```

## Visualization

Line chart (fail_rate over time by destination), stacked bar (attempts vs failures), table of sample error messages.

## References

- [Argo CD — Notifications](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/notifications/)
